body_txt: An older character, who is seen as stodgy, virtuous, or law-abiding. We eventually learn that, in their youth, they were rather less so. When the older character is a mentor figure to the protagonist, The Reveal that they fit this trope can sometimes be part of the process of turning them into a Cool Old Guy . It's also related to Mysterious Past , though there doesn't have to be any mystery involved. Rule-Abiding Rebel or Bourgeois Bohemian can be the result if the character tries to have it both ways. And while not strictly required for this trope, it is very common for the former rebel to now be a parent themselves, juxtaposing their past behavior with the responsibilities of parenthood. This can lead to a case of Generation Xerox if the former rebel now has rebel kids of their own. Overcompensating for their rebellious past by being stern with their kids may increase the likelihood of their kids turning out just like them while increasing the children's shock when they find out what Mom and/or Dad were like at their age. Bonus points if the former teen rebel's own parents are shown taking satisfaction in watching Laser-Guided Karma in action. People who were rebellious in their youth — especially if their actions included a strong sexual element — are said to have been "sowing their wild oats." (This is also said in the present tense of older philanderers.) The etymology of the phrase is explained here. "Sowing one's wild oats" is often a great understatement of reality. In rare instances you might get to see this evolution depicted if the work covers enough time, such as by having a significant Time Skip , being that long of a Long Runner , or getting a Sequel Series or Revival down the line. Definitely Truth in Television . Contrast with Used to Be a Sweet Kid where a villainous or otherwise unpleasant character is shown to be much nicer and polite as a child. Compare Parental Hypocrisy . Teens Are Monsters is often a strong factor of why the character used to be a teen rebel, which can lead to a Teenage Wasteland . Subtrope of Teen Rebellion . See also Retired Outlaw .
